role: proof
locale: en
of_concept: dense-generator-of-torus
source_book: gtm020
source_chapter: "9"
source_section: "8"
---

**Proof.** The subgroup generated by $a$ is $\{ka \bmod \mathbf{Z}^n : k \in \mathbf{Z}\}$. By Kronecker's theorem on simultaneous diophantine approximation, if $1, a_1, \ldots, a_n$ are $\mathbf{Q}$-linearly independent, then the set $\{(k a_1, \ldots, k a_n) \bmod \mathbf{Z}^n : k \in \mathbf{Z}\}$ is dense in $\mathbf{R}^n/\mathbf{Z}^n$. The closure of this cyclic subgroup is a closed subgroup of $T^n$. If it were a proper subgroup, its preimage in $\mathbf{R}^n$ would be a proper closed subgroup containing $\mathbf{Z}^n$, which by the linear independence condition would force a $\mathbf{Q}$-linear relation among $1, a_1, \ldots, a_n$, a contradiction. Hence the generated subgroup is dense. $\square$
